description:
A `
` raw-content element that reaches EOF before its matching `
` fires E-CTX-001 against the unterminated opener (§4.17 Closer recovery).
rationale: §4.17 (Raw-content elements — `<pre>` and `<code>`), Closer recovery: "If EOF is reached before the matching close tag, the block splitter SHALL emit `E-CTX-001` against the unterminated opener and emit the markup block with `closerForm: \"inferred\"`." `<pre>` is a raw-content element, so its body is scanned as a single text run terminated only by the matching close tag; EOF arrives first, so the opener is unterminated and E-CTX-001 SHALL fire. Presence only — the blame span, message text, and the recovered `closerForm: "inferred"` block shape are impl freedom and are not asserted.
